verdicts.title: /api/sync bulk trip_days loop has NO authorization gate — cross-tenant day write/inject (IDOR sibling of the R2 /api/days fix)
verdicts.reasoning: data.py:795-838 INSERTs/UPSERTs trip_days using d['tripId'] verbatim with the ONLY guard being `WHERE trip_days.deleted_at IS NULL` — no `if d['tripId'] not in editable_trip_ids: continue` like the trips loop (302-306) or expenses loop, and there is no test_sync_..._trip_days_loop_blocks_cross_trip_hijack (only the per-row days route and the expenses sync loop have such tests), so any authenticated caller can overwrite/inject days on trips they don't belong to (AI day ids are predictable via useAiPlan.ts:466).

verdicts.title: Deleting a day silently RESURRECTS its pruned photos/documents — server delete_day media cascade undone by the client's loss-free union merge
verdicts.reasoning: deleteDay (handlers.ts:289-322) filters STATE.tripDays but never prunes trip.photos/documents/markedPlaces of the deleted dayId nor calls fetchTripMedia, and _mergeMediaField (media.ts:62-73) is a pure union with zero tombstone awareness, so a media write after the delete re-POSTs the still-local pruned items back over the server's cascade — but it's conditional (a plain fetchTripMedia with no parked write OVERWRITES local arrays at media.ts:231-234 and self-heals, and the resurrected items are the user's own bytes, not a true loss), so P2 not P1.

verdicts.title: Parallel renumber upserts collide on UNIQUE(trip_id, day_number) after deleting a middle day → numbering gap + spurious 'another device updated this day' toast
verdicts.reasoning: handlers.ts:296-320 renumbers survivors to 1..N then fires `Promise.all(...map(upsertDay))` (unordered), the partial UNIQUE idx_trip_days_trip_day_number(trip_id,day_number) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL exists (migration c9d0e1f2a3b4), and a downward-shift upsert can hit an as-yet-unrenumbered live row → IntegrityError → days.py:177-180 returns 409 → api.ts:453-462 only toasts+pullFromServer with NO corrected-number retry, leaving a gap until reload.

verdicts.title: Read-only day modal (dayViewModal) is only half-internationalised — plan slot labels, notes labels and empty-states are hardcoded English
verdicts.reasoning: dayViewModal.ts hardcodes 'Day' (68,72), 'View only' (70), 'Nothing planned.' (51), 'Morning'/'Afternoon'/'Evening' (79,83,87), 'Personal Notes'/'No notes.' (93,94) as literals while only the photos/documents/expert-tip section calls t(), and grep confirms no en.ts keys exist for these strings.

verdicts.title: Journaling modal is entirely hardcoded English (no i18n at all)
verdicts.reasoning: journalingModal.ts has no `import { t }` and uses raw English literals throughout (lines 31-36: 'Journaling','Capture your memories...','What happened today?...','Save Story','Close') plus the 'Memories saved!' toast at line 45, and grep finds none of these strings in en.ts.

verdicts.title: Day-pin flow alerts are hardcoded English
verdicts.reasoning: handlers.ts:111 ('Click on the map to set the location...'), 174 ('Location saved!'), and 321 ('Day deleted') pass raw English to showLiquidAlert while the same file uses t() for delete-confirm and the Trip-Hub error, and grep confirms no matching en.ts keys exist.

verdicts.title: Read-only day modal shows a blue 'Day 0' badge for the Trip Hub anchor instead of a Trip Hub chip
verdicts.reasoning: dayViewModal.ts:68 unconditionally renders `Day ${day.dayNumber}` and the title falls back to `Day ${dayNumber}` (72), while only the photos/documents sections special-case dayNumber===0 — the isAnchor gold-chip + 'Trip Hub' title branching in dayDetailModal.ts:238-249 was never ported.

verdicts.title: Shortlist 'Add to AM/PM/Eve' ✓ marker and toggle use naive case-insensitive substring matching — false positives for short/substring place names corrupt the wrong plan line
verdicts.reasoning: Presence is `planVals[time].includes(place.name.toLowerCase())` (dayDetailModal.ts:744) and removal is `lines.findIndex(l => l.toLowerCase().includes(needle))`+splice (862-863) with no word-boundary/whole-line check while the add format is '- {name}', so a short name that is a substring of another line false-positives the ✓ and strips/mismatches the wrong line (the code comments even acknowledge the loose substring behavior).

verdicts.title: Dropping a day pin on the map doesn't persist to local storage until 'Save pin' — navigating away loses the placed pin
verdicts.reasoning: addDayPin's map-click listener (handlers.ts:113-119) sets day.lat/lon/lng and calls navigate() but never emit('state:changed'), whereas cancelPinEdit (161), saveDayPin (172) and deleteDayPin (189) all emit — so the dropped coords live only in memory until saveDayPin runs upsertDay, and a reload before saving loses them.
